faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Fordham home?
faqs.answer: Signs include small holes in wool clothing, silk fabrics, or upholstery; webbing in closet corners; small cream-colored larvae on fabrics; and adult moths flying near wardrobes. You may also notice damaged rugs, blankets, or fur items. If you spot any of these signs in your Fordham residence, contact us immediately for an inspection.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth control cost in Fordham?
faqs.answer: Our clothes moth control services in Fordham range from $550 to $5,000 depending on the size of your home, severity of the infestation, number of treatment areas, and whether follow-up visits are needed. A typical apartment treatment starts around $550-$900, while larger homes or severe infestations requiring multiple treatments can cost more.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take to work?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment takes 1-3 hours depending on your property size. You'll see immediate reduction in adult moth activity, but complete elimination requires 2-4 weeks as eggs and larvae are addressed. Most Fordham clients need 2-3 treatments spaced several weeks apart to break the moth life cycle and ensure all stages are eliminated.

faqs.question: Are clothes moth treatments safe for my family and fabrics in Fordham?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use treatments specifically designed to eliminate clothes moths while being safe for humans, pets, and your valuable textiles. Our technicians apply targeted solutions to affected areas and provide detailed instructions for fabric cleaning and storage. We take extra care with delicate items and vintage clothing common in Fordham's historic homes.
